name: parallax
description: Parallax — independent paths, one verified result. A spec-driven, parallel, blind-coder TDD pipeline: a maximally concrete read-only spec drives two independent tracks (a test-writer and a blind-coder that never sees the tests); a single whole-seeing arbiter runs the tests and loops with failure analysis until green, then pushes a feature branch. Optionally adds an independent cross-model verifier — a provider chain of non-Claude models (Codex primary, optional fallbacks like Gemini, CLI or API) — that adversarially reviews the spec before the blind tracks and each green slice after (opt-in via .parallax/codex.toml). An autonomous driver (/parallax:auto, --autonomous --from-doc) runs the whole cycle from a written brief with no human gates — independent slices build in parallel waves, the cross-model verifier replaces the human gate, and anything genuinely ambiguous is parked for review; headless and schedulable, and nothing reaches main without a human. On a usage limit the verifier falls back to the next provider, and if all are limited the run checkpoints and resumes from the same place on an hourly retry, losing no progress. Optional Telegram notifications report the autonomous run's progress (verbose) or just its lifecycle and limit/resume events. Runs in Claude Code on subscription limits. v0.31 adds /parallax:resolve — a human-decided safe completion that turns a parked spec-gap into a new contract generation with full invalidation and a fresh blind rebuild (never a 'ship anyway'), plus a mandatory Existing Affordance Review in /parallax:spec that records why a thin overlay or a new module was chosen, preventing needless new subsystems. v0.32 adds a lightweight Architecture Fitness check in /parallax:spec that catches common AI maintainability failures before freeze (wrong seam, shallow wrapper, duplicated business logic, speculative adapter, no regression seam, ignored local architecture contract) — blocking only on a concrete consequence, never on style. v0.33 adds an optional bounded Project Scout fanout inside /parallax:spec for large/unknown repos: read-only internal scouts gather cited evidence (existing seams, local architecture contracts, testing seams, source-of-truth, risky integrations) which the main agent verifies before relying on — no new command, scouts never decide, linear flow stays the default. v0.34 adds an Intake / AI-architect handoff layer: /parallax:spec --from-doc and /parallax:auto understand a structured Parallax Brief Packet (problem, behavior, constraints, proposed shape, evidence, non-goals) but treat any proposed architecture as a hypothesis — still running the Existing Affordance Review, Architecture Fitness and the usual pre-freeze gates; if a brief isn't build-ready, Parallax returns a bounded Intake Response with blocking questions instead of guessing and never starts the build. No new command. v0.35 is a measurement release: it adds an evaluation-harness v2 under bench/ (record/fixture/aggregate schemas, static maintainability metrics, aggregation, and pilot external-benchmark adapters for SWE-bench / Aider / Code Review Bench) to honestly measure false-green, overbuild, wrong-seam, scout and intake behaviour ahead of the v0.36 benchmark — no new command and no runtime behaviour change or claim. v0.36 hardens live-run evidence: every /parallax:spec, :run, :auto and :resolve maintains structured, plugin-version-stamped artifacts under .parallax/<slug>/evidence/ (a run-evidence.json, an append-only events.jsonl timeline, and optional e2e-checks and defect-loop ledgers) so a real run leaves first-class auditable evidence instead of needing transcript reconstruction — auditability only, no new command, no benchmark or quality claim. v0.36.1 tightens those four evidence JSON Schemas so they mechanically require the TZ minimum fields they previously only described: events must carry artifact_paths (even if empty), run-evidence must include its repo, artifacts, capabilities_exercised and evidence_limits fields (null where data is unavailable), and a defect-loop must carry the full source→spec→test→fix→re-verify chain — a schema-strictness fix only, with no behaviour, UX or command change. v0.37 is a runtime-governance hardening release that turns safety-critical rules which relied on orchestrator discipline into mechanical gates, with no new public command and no benchmark or quality claim: a deterministic blindfold guard (scripts/blindfold-guard.py) fails closed when implementation/compiled output leaks into the test worktree or tests leak into the coder worktree, re-checked per wave; a standalone finalize gate (scripts/finalize-gate.py) refuses feature push / epic advance unless every slice carries a committed green arbiter receipt, no slice is green-unverified, the run evidence is committed and the run-state is fresh, delegating the deep checks to epic-gate.py; a whole-feature invariant sweep (scripts/feature-sweep.py) catches cross-file PII/money/dead-seam/mock-only violations a per-slice green misses; and an auditable contract-tightening guard (scripts/contract-amend.py) rejects any post-freeze contract edit that is not a sanctioned, evidence-backed mechanical-tightening amendment that bumps the contract hash — every v0.31–v0.36.1 boundary preserved. v0.37.1 hardens the finalize gate's freshness check: run-state now carries a terminal completion receipt (completed_at, run_id, verified_tree, and the sha256 of the committed run-evidence.json and events.jsonl, plus a run_completed terminal event), required once status is complete, and finalize-gate.py recomputes those hashes and the code-tree hash from the committed feature ref and matches them — so 'fresh' means the terminal run-state, terminal evidence, terminal event and verified code tree all agree, not merely a non-empty updated_at; mechanical evidence binding only, no new command and no benchmark or quality claim. v0.37.2 corrects the /parallax:run finalization ORDER so the remote feature-branch push happens only after finalize-gate.py passes on the pinned VERIFIED_OID (v0.37.1's prompt-contract pushed the feature before the gate); the finalize-gate hold now states 'feature NOT pushed; epic NOT advanced', and a harness ordering guard fails if the feature push appears before the gate — a prompt-contract ordering fix only, no behaviour/command/claim change. v0.37.3 is a live-run reliability hardening patch driven by three real production monorepo runs (recorded in references/live-run-audit-findings.md — a findings list, not a benchmark): blindfold-guard.py gains a slice-scoped monorepo mode — a schema-validated per-slice scope manifest keeps sibling-package roots and the existing base tree resolvable on the test side while the slice's own new/changed implementation still fails closed, a whole-tree allowlist is schema-rejected, and .parallax/** plus ordinary bin/ scripts stop false-positiving as leaks; pre-freeze closure becomes mechanical — pre-freeze-state.json carries a closure object only scripts/pre-freeze-budget.py can move to independent-pass, and only on a schema-valid verifier pass, so an orchestrator/human self-attestation can never certify a freeze, and plain --from-doc without --autonomous unambiguously keeps the human OK gate; merge-ledger.py canonicalizes finding paths against the repo's tracked files so a basename echo binds to the same finding instead of minting a phantom duplicate (ambiguous basenames stay distinct with a loud warning); run-phase evidence becomes first-class — scripts/evidence-event.py appends schema-valid arbiter-iteration / codex-round / slice-green / PR / merge / handoff events and moves run-evidence.json off frozen-spec, so events.jsonl no longer stops at spec_frozen; the arbiter/spec/run/test-writer contracts add a directive user-reachability bar for frontend seams (interaction proof — drive the entry affordance and assert the destination content appears — never router membership alone); and the codex-judge transport is hardened (canonical codex exec ends in < /dev/null, top-level-allOf schemas reach providers as a stripped copy while the full schema stays the acceptance bar, and a silent timeout is never classified as a rate limit). No new public command, no benchmark or quality claim, every v0.31–v0.37.2 boundary preserved. v0.37.4 adds a z.ai (GLM) verifier provider so the cross-model verifier keeps working when the Codex CLI is unavailable or rate-limited: an OpenAI-compatible HTTP-API fallback (form="api") that needs no CLI, configured in .parallax/codex.toml with base_url + a key_env naming the env var that holds ZAI_API_KEY (the secret is never committed — put it in an untracked .parallax/zai.env and source it), returning a schema-validated verdict exactly like the existing Gemini-API path; the codex→z.ai chain is the documented default, provider values stay directive (codex | gemini | zai), and no runtime behaviour changes when the fallback is absent — additive and opt-in, no new command, no benchmark or quality claim. v0.37.5 is a governance self-attestation hardening release (minor-scope, patch-numbered) driven by two real v0.37.4 production runs (creatives-core COMPLETE, linkedin-selfservice-bot RUNNING — a findings list, not a benchmark): the F3 self-attestation threat model recurred in three mechanically-uncovered shapes and each is now a fail-closed gate. (1) Freeze-gate MODE BINDING: pre-freeze state pins mode.autonomous, pre-freeze-budget.py freeze-check refuses an autonomous freeze without closure.status=independent-pass — a human at the console can no longer open the interactive human-OK side door for an --autonomous run, grant-one refuses under autonomous, and on_missing=warn no longer licenses an autonomous freeze. (2) PINNED round budget: pin-policy freezes the [review] policy at spec-freeze into review-policy.frozen.json; merge-ledger, triage --pinned-policy and epic-gate evaluate rounds against the PIN — the live bypass (sed codex.toml max_rounds 2->3 + re-stamp all ledgers) now fails closed twice over, and widening is sanctioned only by a recorded review-budget amendment (contract-amend.py record-budget, human-repeated machine-minted token; the contract-bytes chain stays mechanical-tightening-only). (3) Post-green RECEIPT integrity: every verifier round persists the verbatim provider verdict as reviews/S<n>.round<r>.raw.json (symmetric with pre-freeze); merge-ledger ingests only a schema-valid round whose raw equals it and records {round, raw_artifact, raw_sha256} receipts; triage and epic-gate refuse uncovered rounds and re-derive committed raws — a malformed envelope is a provider error, never material for a hand-authored pass. Plus: resume-reconcile.py makes git the truth over run-state tips across session boundaries (write-back + session_handoff, F7); feature-sweep consumers must ride the PRODUCTION path (a test-authored duplicate is not a consumer) and the sweep is receipted (sweep-receipt.json, required by finalize-gate — 'clean' as prose is not proof); finalize-gate self-audits iteration undercount; the lease is real-or-dropped and transcript provenance must name the .jsonl itself; and strip-openai-schema.py emits a fully OpenAI-strict call copy (required everywhere, additionalProperties:false, nullable optionals) with a normalize step so the FULL schema stays the acceptance bar — zero hand-tuned schema retries. No new public command, no capability, no benchmark or quality claim; every v0.31–v0.37.4 boundary preserved. v0.38.0 adds --adopt: /parallax:run --adopt <slug> (and /parallax:auto --adopt) recovers a run interrupted UNCLEANLY — a session/context death mid-build with in-flight background tracks — distinct from the existing clean --resume limit-pause; it reconstructs ground truth git-first from a new per-slug dispatched-subagent manifest (subagents.json), the v0.37.5-reconciled checkpoint and events.jsonl, reaps in-flight background branches, re-dispatches only genuinely-missing tracks blind, and fails closed (escalates) on irreconcilable state — never fabricating a track or marking a slice done without its receipts; a machine-generated handoff.md replaces the hand-written RUN-HANDOFF an operator needed before. Capability release; public claims stay design-intent until a production soak exercises adopt; no auto-push to main, no new top-level command, every v0.31–v0.37.5 boundary preserved. v0.39.0 is a hardening minor that makes the already-shipped fail-closed gates fire on the REAL box (hand-driven / monorepo / CI), not only in the harness — the v0.38.1 monorepo soak found every production run was hand-driven so the gates never executed: a hand-driven/degraded finalize (a --finalize flag on /parallax:run's done-gate, NOT a new command, via scripts/finalize-handdriven.py) emits the adopt-critical evidence + runs evidence-event.py audit-slice fail-closed, routes a hand-committed post-green verdict through the merge-ledger/triage schema-gate, and refuses a stale tip; monorepo silent-failure guards make a zero-match blindfold git-rm fail closed (blindfold-guard.py assert-pathspec-match) and add a pre-push git fetch + merge-base --is-ancestor + branch==HEAD guard (scripts/push-guard.sh) mechanizing the detached-HEAD check operators did by hand; plus CI/lint parity (a validation-contract ci_equivalent whole-tree form so local-green == CI-green, scripts/ci-parity.py), a fail-closed fix for a new impl file absent from protected_impl_paths reachable via a broad dep-glob (blindfold-guard.py --base-ref), done-gate telemetry regeneration (run-evidence version/status restamp), and CLI foot-gun fixes (triage.py --schema resolves vs __file__, pre-freeze-budget.py record accepts a JSON string or path). No new command, no new behavior thesis, every v0.31–v0.38.1 boundary preserved; the public 'gates protect production' claim stays out until one clean monorepo skill-flow (or adopt) soak runs.
